tips: Provide customer data (firmographics and behavior). Copilot will cluster and characterize segments. Output drives campaign strategy and resource allocation.

title: Sourcing Strategy Development
prompt_text: Develop sourcing strategy for [CATEGORY]: 1) Market Analysis (supplier landscape and pricing trends), 2) Current State (suppliers and spend and performance ratings), 3) Target State (sourcing approach and preferred supplier mix), 4) Negotiation Plan, 5) Risk Mitigation Strategy.
description: Consolidates supplier intelligence and category strategy into an actionable procurement plan.
tips: Gather RFx responses and historical spend data and supplier scorecards. Copilot will structure supplier options and identify savings opportunities.

title: Contract Lifecycle Tracker
prompt_text: Track contract portfolio: 1) Summary (total contracts and spend and renewal schedule), 2) Contract Schedule (table: Vendor | Category | Value | Renewal Date | Status), 3) Upcoming Renewals flagged by priority, 4) Risk Assessment per vendor, 5) Negotiation Priorities ranked by value.
description: Single source of truth for contract renewals and negotiations with proactive deadline management.
tips: Export from contract management system (Icertis or Coupa) or maintain in Excel. Copilot will organize and highlight expiring contracts and flag risks.

title: Change Management Communication Plan
prompt_text: I am rolling out [CHANGE_INITIATIVE] affecting [AUDIENCE]. Draft a change management communication plan with: 1) Stakeholder Map (who needs to know what when), 2) Key Messages per audience tier, 3) Communication Timeline (pre-launch / launch week / post-launch), 4) FAQ document for managers, 5) Resistance mitigation talking points.
description: Creates structured change comms that address different stakeholder needs and proactively manage resistance.
tips: Provide the change details and impacted groups. Copilot will tailor messaging by audience. Include the business rationale — people accept change better when they understand why.

title: Negotiation Preparation Brief
prompt_text: I am preparing to negotiate [DEAL_TYPE] with [COUNTERPARTY]. Help me prepare: 1) Their likely priorities and constraints, 2) Our BATNA (best alternative), 3) Trading strategies (what we offer vs. what we need), 4) Recommended opening position, 5) Walk-away point, 6) Top 5 objections they might raise with counter-arguments.
description: Structured negotiation prep covering strategy and tactics and contingency scenarios.
tips: Provide context on past interactions and relationship dynamics. Copilot will tailor strategy. Practice your opening statement out loud before the meeting.

title: Root Cause Analysis Framework
prompt_text: I have encountered [PROBLEM_DESCRIPTION]. Walk me through a root cause analysis: 1) Symptom Documentation, 2) Timeline (when did it start and what changed), 3) Scope (what is affected vs. what is not), 4) Top 3 theories with diagnostic questions for each, 5) Most likely root cause with evidence, 6) Recommended fixes ranked by effort vs. impact.
description: Structured problem-solving that prevents treating symptoms instead of addressing actual causes.
tips: Describe symptoms in specific detail. Copilot will ask probing questions. Resist jumping to solutions before validating root cause.

title: Stakeholder Alignment Document
prompt_text: I need to align [STAKEHOLDERS] on [INITIATIVE]. Create a one-page alignment document with: 1) Problem Statement (2-3 sentences), 2) Proposed Solution, 3) Expected Outcomes with metrics, 4) Resource Requirements, 5) Timeline and milestones, 6) Decision needed and by when.
description: Ensures all stakeholders have the same understanding of what is being proposed and what decision is required.
tips: Keep it to one page. Decision-makers scan — use bold headers and keep each section to 2-3 sentences max. End with a clear ask.

title: Process Documentation Template
prompt_text: Document the process for [PROCESS_NAME]: 1) Process Owner and Stakeholders, 2) Trigger (what starts this process), 3) Step-by-step procedure with decision points, 4) Roles and Responsibilities (RACI), 5) Systems and Tools used, 6) Exceptions and escalation paths, 7) SLA and metrics.
description: Captures institutional knowledge into reusable process documentation that survives team changes.
tips: Walk through the process mentally before documenting. Flag any steps where tribal knowledge is needed. Include screenshots or references where helpful.

title: Board Meeting Prep Package
prompt_text: Prepare a board meeting prep package for [TOPIC]: 1) Executive Brief (one paragraph), 2) Key Metrics Dashboard (table: Metric | Target | Actual | Trend), 3) Strategic Updates (3-5 bullet points), 4) Risks and Mitigations, 5) Decision Items with recommendations, 6) Appendix with supporting data.
description: Creates executive-grade board materials that are concise and decision-oriented.
tips: Board members have limited time. Lead with conclusions not context. Use tables over paragraphs. Flag any items requiring a vote or approval.

title: Vendor Evaluation Scorecard
prompt_text: Evaluate [VENDORS] for [REQUIREMENT]. Create a weighted scorecard: 1) Evaluation Criteria (table: Criterion | Weight | Vendor A | Vendor B | Vendor C), 2) Scoring methodology, 3) Strengths and weaknesses per vendor, 4) Total weighted score, 5) Recommendation with rationale.
description: Structured vendor comparison that makes procurement decisions transparent and defensible.
tips: Define criteria and weights before scoring. Include both quantitative (price and SLA) and qualitative (innovation and cultural fit) factors.

title: Workshop Facilitation Guide
prompt_text: Design a [DURATION] workshop on [TOPIC] for [AUDIENCE]. Include: 1) Objectives and success metrics, 2) Agenda with timing (table: Time | Activity | Method | Materials), 3) Discussion prompts for each segment, 4) Breakout group instructions, 5) Parking lot template, 6) Follow-up action plan template.
description: Creates a ready-to-run workshop plan with timing and discussion prompts and facilitation notes.
tips: Test timing by running through agenda mentally. Build in 10% buffer time. Have a backup activity if discussion runs short. Always end with clear next steps.

title: Crisis Communication Playbook
prompt_text: Develop a crisis communication plan for [SCENARIO]: 1) Situation Assessment (severity and scope and stakeholders), 2) Internal Communication (who needs to know in what order), 3) External Messaging (holding statement and Q&A), 4) Social Media Response Protocol, 5) Spokesperson Prep (talking points and dos and donts), 6) Recovery Communication Plan.
description: Prepares teams for crisis situations with pre-built messaging and escalation protocols.
tips: Adapt to your specific crisis type. Have legal review external messaging. Speed matters in crisis — have templates ready before you need them.

title: Knowledge Transfer Checklist
prompt_text: Create a knowledge transfer plan for [DEPARTING_ROLE]: 1) Critical Knowledge Inventory (what only this person knows), 2) Document List (what needs to be written down), 3) Handoff Sessions (table: Topic | Recipient | Date | Status), 4) Access and Credentials Transfer, 5) Relationship Map (key contacts and context), 6) Open Items and Risks.
description: Ensures institutional knowledge is captured before team transitions to prevent operational gaps.
tips: Start knowledge transfer 4-6 weeks before departure. Prioritize undocumented tribal knowledge. Have the departing person review the final document.

title: Quarterly Business Review Summary
prompt_text: Summarize this quarterly business review for [DIVISION]: Key metrics vs. targets (table format), top 3 wins, top 3 challenges, strategic initiatives status (On Track/At Risk/Off Track), and recommended priorities for next quarter. Keep to 2 pages max.
description: Condenses lengthy QBR materials into an executive-ready summary highlighting what matters most.
tips: Provide the raw QBR data or deck. Copilot will extract the signal from the noise. Focus on variance from plan rather than restating the plan.

title: Contract Key Terms Summary
prompt_text: Review this [CONTRACT_TYPE] and extract: 1) Key Terms (table: Clause | Summary | Risk Level), 2) Financial Obligations (amounts and dates and conditions), 3) Termination Provisions, 4) Liability and Indemnification, 5) Renewal Terms, 6) Red Flags or unusual clauses.
description: Extracts critical contract terms so decision-makers can review without reading the full document.
tips: Upload the contract or paste key sections. Copilot will flag non-standard clauses. Always have legal review before signing — this is a summary not legal advice.

title: OKR Framework Builder
prompt_text: Help me build OKRs for [TEAM_OR_INITIATIVE] for [PERIOD]. For each Objective: 1) Objective statement (qualitative and inspiring), 2) 3-5 Key Results (quantitative and measurable), 3) Initiatives that drive each KR, 4) Owner and timeline. Ensure OKRs are ambitious but achievable.
description: Creates well-structured OKRs that connect team work to strategic priorities with clear measurement.
tips: Start with 2-3 objectives max. Key Results should be outcomes not activities. Use the formula: Verb + Metric + From X to Y by Date.

title: Decision Log & RACI Matrix
prompt_text: Create a decision framework for [PROJECT_OR_INITIATIVE]: 1) Decision Log (table: Decision | Date | Made By | Rationale | Status), 2) RACI Matrix (table: Activity | Responsible | Accountable | Consulted | Informed), 3) Escalation Path for unresolved decisions, 4) Review cadence.
description: Brings structure to decision-making and accountability across complex projects.
tips: Define RACI before kickoff. Only one person should be Accountable per row. Review the decision log monthly to ensure consistency.

title: Data Quality Assessment
prompt_text: Assess data quality for [DATASET_OR_SYSTEM]: 1) Completeness (% of missing values by field), 2) Accuracy (known errors or inconsistencies), 3) Timeliness (data freshness and update frequency), 4) Consistency (cross-system alignment), 5) Recommendations (prioritized fixes with effort estimate).
description: Provides a structured data quality health check to inform cleanup priorities and data governance.
tips: Provide sample data or describe the schema. Copilot can analyze patterns and flag anomalies. Pair with a data steward for remediation planning.

title: Policy Exception Request
prompt_text: Draft a policy exception request for [POLICY_NAME]: 1) Current Policy Statement, 2) Requested Exception (what and why and for how long), 3) Business Justification with impact if denied, 4) Risk Assessment and proposed mitigations, 5) Approval Chain and timeline needed.
description: Creates a well-structured exception request that balances business need with risk management.
tips: Include the specific policy clause number. Quantify the business impact. Propose a time-bound exception with review date rather than permanent change.

title: Post-Mortem / Retrospective Template
prompt_text: Facilitate a post-mortem for [INCIDENT_OR_PROJECT]: 1) Timeline of Events, 2) What Went Well (keep doing), 3) What Went Wrong (stop doing), 4) Root Causes (not just symptoms), 5) Action Items (table: Action | Owner | Due Date | Priority), 6) Lessons Learned for future reference.
description: Turns failures and successes into organizational learning with clear accountability for improvements.
tips: No blame — focus on systems and processes not individuals. Capture actions with deadlines or they will not happen. Share findings broadly.

title: Executive Briefing One-Pager
prompt_text: Create an executive one-pager on [TOPIC]: Structure as: Header (title and date and author), Situation (2-3 sentences), Complication (what changed or what is the challenge), Resolution (recommended path), Key Metrics (small table), Next Steps and Ask. Keep under 500 words.
description: Distills complex topics into the concise format executives actually read and act on.
tips: Use the SCR framework (Situation Complication Resolution). Lead with the ask not the background. Executives decide — give them what they need to decide.

title: Operational Playbook Section
prompt_text: Write a playbook section for [OPERATIONAL_SCENARIO]: 1) Trigger Conditions (when does this apply), 2) Step-by-Step Response Procedure, 3) Decision Tree for key branch points, 4) Escalation Criteria and contacts, 5) Communication Templates (internal and external), 6) Recovery Steps.
description: Creates standardized operational procedures that reduce response time and ensure consistency.
tips: Test the playbook with a tabletop exercise. Include contact info that is kept current. Version control the document and set review dates.

title: Innovation Proposal Framework
prompt_text: Structure an innovation proposal for [IDEA]: 1) Problem Statement and market opportunity, 2) Proposed Solution (how it works), 3) Competitive Landscape, 4) Business Model (revenue and cost and margin), 5) MVP Scope and timeline, 6) Resource Ask, 7) Success Metrics and kill criteria.
description: Packages innovation ideas into a format that leadership can evaluate and fund.
tips: Be honest about unknowns. Include kill criteria — knowing when to stop is as important as knowing when to start. Show customer validation if available.

title: Interview Scorecard System
prompt_text: Design an interview scorecard for [ROLE]: 1) Core Competencies to assess (table: Competency | Definition | Behavioral Indicators), 2) Scoring Rubric (1-5 scale with anchors), 3) Interview Questions mapped to competencies, 4) Red Flags to watch for, 5) Overall Assessment Template.
description: Creates consistent and defensible interview evaluation across all interviewers for a role.
tips: Align competencies with job description. Train interviewers on the rubric before use. Score independently before debrief to avoid groupthink.

title: Skills Gap Analysis & Learning Plan
prompt_text: Conduct a skills gap analysis for [TEAM_OR_ROLE]: 1) Required Skills Matrix (table: Skill | Required Level | Current Level | Gap), 2) Priority Skills (ranked by business impact), 3) Learning Interventions per skill, 4) Timeline and milestones, 5) Budget estimate, 6) Success Metrics.
description: Identifies and prioritizes skill development needs with actionable learning pathways.
tips: Survey the team for self-assessment and cross-reference with manager ratings. Focus on skills that drive business outcomes not just nice-to-haves.

title: Employee Engagement Action Plan
prompt_text: Based on [SURVEY_RESULTS_OR_FEEDBACK], create an engagement action plan: 1) Key Findings Summary, 2) Root Cause Analysis for bottom 3 scores, 3) Action Items (table: Theme | Action | Owner | Timeline | Success Metric), 4) Communication Plan to employees, 5) Follow-up measurement approach.
description: Turns engagement survey data into concrete improvements with accountability and measurement.
tips: Address the top 3 issues — do not try to fix everything at once. Communicate back to employees what you heard and what you are doing about it.

title: Brand Voice Guidelines
prompt_text: Develop brand voice guidelines for [BRAND]: 1) Brand Personality (3-5 traits with definitions), 2) Voice Characteristics (table: We Are | We Are Not), 3) Tone Spectrum by channel (social vs. formal vs. support), 4) Writing Do's and Don'ts with examples, 5) Sample copy for key touchpoints.
description: Codifies brand voice so all content creators produce consistent messaging across channels.
tips: Include real before/after examples. Test guidelines with a team member who was not involved in creating them. Review quarterly as brand evolves.

title: A/B Testing Strategy
prompt_text: Design an A/B testing plan for [CAMPAIGN_OR_PAGE]: 1) Hypothesis (what we believe and why), 2) Test Variables (what changes between A and B), 3) Success Metrics (primary and secondary), 4) Sample Size calculation, 5) Test Duration, 6) Statistical Significance threshold, 7) Decision Framework (what we do with results).
description: Creates rigorous testing plans that generate actionable insights rather than inconclusive data.
tips: Test one variable at a time. Ensure sample size is large enough for significance. Define success criteria before running the test — do not move goalposts.

title: Marketing Attribution Model
prompt_text: Build a marketing attribution analysis for [CAMPAIGN_PERIOD]: 1) Channel Performance (table: Channel | Spend | Leads | Conversions | CAC | ROAS), 2) Attribution Model Comparison (first-touch vs. last-touch vs. multi-touch), 3) Channel Efficiency Rankings, 4) Budget Reallocation Recommendations, 5) Confidence Level for each recommendation.
description: Provides data-driven guidance for marketing budget allocation across channels.
tips: Gather spend and conversion data by channel. Copilot will model different attribution approaches. Multi-touch is usually most accurate but requires more data.

title: Workplace Experience Program
prompt_text: Design a workplace experience program for [LOCATION]: 1) Current State Assessment (employee satisfaction and utilization), 2) Program Pillars (e.g. collaboration spaces/wellness/technology), 3) Initiative Roadmap (table: Initiative | Cost | Timeline | Expected Impact), 4) Success Metrics, 5) Feedback Loop mechanism.
description: Creates a holistic workplace improvement plan that drives employee satisfaction and space utilization.
tips: Survey employees first to understand what they actually want. Prioritize quick wins alongside longer-term investments. Measure before and after.

title: Sustainability & ESG Reporting
prompt_text: Compile sustainability metrics for [PORTFOLIO_OR_BUILDING]: 1) Energy Consumption (kWh per sq ft and YoY trend), 2) Carbon Footprint (Scope 1/2/3), 3) Water Usage, 4) Waste Diversion Rate, 5) Green Certifications Status, 6) Improvement Initiatives with projected impact, 7) ESG Reporting alignment.
description: Consolidates environmental performance data for ESG reporting and sustainability planning.
tips: Gather utility bills and waste hauler reports. Copilot will calculate metrics and compare to benchmarks. Align with GRI or TCFD frameworks as applicable.

title: Supplier Performance Review
prompt_text: Conduct a supplier performance review for [SUPPLIER]: 1) Scorecard (table: KPI | Target | Actual | Trend), 2) Delivery Performance (on-time and in-full rates), 3) Quality Metrics (defect rate and returns), 4) Relationship Assessment, 5) Improvement Actions with deadlines, 6) Continuation Recommendation.
description: Provides a structured supplier assessment framework for quarterly business reviews.
tips: Use actual performance data not perceptions. Compare against contract SLAs. Share the scorecard with the supplier as a collaboration tool.

title: Spend Analysis & Category Review
prompt_text: Analyze spend for [CATEGORY]: 1) Total Spend Overview (by supplier and by business unit and by period), 2) Supplier Concentration (% of spend with top 5 suppliers), 3) Maverick Spend Identification, 4) Savings Opportunities, 5) Contract Compliance Rate, 6) Recommended Actions.
description: Identifies savings and compliance opportunities through structured spend visibility.
tips: Export AP data for the period. Copilot will categorize and identify patterns. Focus on high-spend categories first for maximum impact.

title: Financial Model Builder
prompt_text: Build a financial model in this workbook for [SCENARIO]: Create sheets for 1) Assumptions (input cells highlighted in blue), 2) Revenue Model (by product line and by month), 3) Cost Structure (fixed vs. variable), 4) P&L Summary (with formulas linking to assumptions), 5) Sensitivity Analysis (what-if on key variables).
description: Creates a linked financial model where changing assumptions automatically flows through to outputs.
tips: Use named ranges for key assumptions. Highlight input cells in blue and output cells in green. Include data validation dropdowns where applicable.

title: Dashboard Data Preparation
prompt_text: Prepare this raw data for dashboard visualization: 1) Clean and standardize column formats, 2) Remove duplicates and handle missing values, 3) Create calculated columns ([SPECIFY_METRICS]), 4) Build pivot table summaries, 5) Add conditional formatting to highlight outliers, 6) Create a summary sheet with key KPIs.
description: Transforms raw data into dashboard-ready format with calculations and formatting.
tips: Describe the final dashboard you want. Copilot will work backwards to structure the data correctly. Keep raw data on a separate sheet from calculations.

title: Budget vs Actual Tracker
prompt_text: Create a budget variance tracker: 1) Monthly columns (Jan-Dec) with Budget and Actual and Variance rows, 2) Conditional formatting (green for favorable and red for unfavorable variance), 3) YTD rollup formulas, 4) Variance % calculation, 5) Chart showing budget vs. actual trend line, 6) Commentary column for explanations.
description: Automates budget tracking with visual variance highlighting and trend analysis.
tips: Enter budget figures first then actuals monthly. Copilot will calculate variances and flag items exceeding threshold. Set a materiality threshold (e.g. 5%).

title: Sales Pipeline Analyzer
prompt_text: Analyze this sales pipeline data: 1) Pipeline Summary (table: Stage | Count | Value | Avg Deal Size | Conversion Rate), 2) Weighted Pipeline Value, 3) Aging Analysis (deals stuck in stage > [DAYS]), 4) Win/Loss Analysis by [SEGMENT], 5) Forecast for next quarter, 6) Rep Performance Comparison.
description: Provides pipeline health metrics and forecasting from raw CRM export data.
tips: Export from CRM as CSV. Copilot will calculate stage conversion rates and weighted values. Flag deals with no activity in 30+ days.

title: Workforce Planning Model
prompt_text: Build a workforce planning model: 1) Current Headcount by department and role and level, 2) Attrition Assumptions (historical turnover rate by segment), 3) Growth Plan (new positions by quarter), 4) Cost Model (fully loaded cost per FTE by level), 5) Gap Analysis (required vs. projected headcount), 6) Hiring Timeline.
description: Models workforce supply and demand with cost implications for budget planning.
tips: Use historical attrition data for accuracy. Include ramp time for new hires. Factor in internal mobility and promotions not just external hiring.

title: Compensation Benchmarking Workbook
prompt_text: Build a compensation benchmarking workbook: 1) Market Data Input (survey sources by role), 2) Internal vs. Market Comparison (table: Role | Internal Median | Market P25/P50/P75 | Compa-Ratio), 3) Equity Analysis by demographics, 4) Adjustment Recommendations, 5) Budget Impact Calculator.
description: Automates comp analysis comparing internal pay to market benchmarks with equity lens.
tips: Import survey data as-is. Copilot will normalize and compare. Include all comp elements (base + bonus + equity) for total rewards view.

title: Operating Expense Forecast
prompt_text: Create an OpEx forecast model for [PORTFOLIO]: 1) Expense Categories (utilities/maintenance/security/cleaning/insurance), 2) Historical Spend (3 years), 3) Inflation Assumptions by category, 4) Forecast (monthly for 12 months), 5) Variance tracking vs. prior year, 6) Scenario toggle (base/optimistic/pessimistic).
description: Models CRE operating costs with inflation assumptions and scenario analysis for budget planning.
tips: Use actual invoices for historical accuracy. Utility costs are seasonal — model monthly not annual averages. Include contracted rate escalations.

title: Campaign ROI Calculator
prompt_text: Build a campaign ROI calculator: 1) Input Sheet (spend by channel and conversion rates and average deal value), 2) Funnel Model (impressions to leads to MQLs to SQLs to closed), 3) ROI by Channel (table: Channel | Spend | Revenue | ROI % | CAC), 4) Break-even Analysis, 5) Scenario Modeling (what-if on conversion rates).
description: Calculates marketing ROI with full-funnel visibility and scenario modeling for budget optimization.
tips: Enter actual conversion rates from analytics. Copilot will build the funnel math. Conservative assumptions are better for credibility with finance.

title: Total Cost of Ownership Calculator
prompt_text: Build a TCO calculator comparing [VENDORS]: 1) Direct Costs (license/purchase and implementation), 2) Indirect Costs (training/change management/productivity loss), 3) Ongoing Costs (maintenance/support/upgrades per year), 4) 3-Year and 5-Year TCO comparison, 5) NPV calculation, 6) Sensitivity analysis on key variables.
description: Provides apples-to-apples vendor cost comparison beyond sticker price including hidden costs.
tips: Include all cost categories even if estimated. The vendor with lowest price is rarely lowest TCO. Document assumptions for audit trail.

title: Inventory Management Tracker
prompt_text: Create an inventory tracking workbook: 1) Item Master (SKU | Description | Category | Unit Cost | Reorder Point), 2) Stock Levels (current quantity and location), 3) Movement Log (date | item | quantity | type: in/out), 4) Alerts for items below reorder point, 5) ABC Analysis (classify by value), 6) Turnover Rate Calculation.
description: Automates inventory tracking with reorder alerts and classification analysis.
tips: Set reorder points based on lead time and demand variability. Copilot will calculate turnover and classify items. Review ABC classification quarterly.

title: Project Resource Allocation
prompt_text: Build a resource allocation model: 1) Team Roster (name | role | availability % | cost rate), 2) Project List (project | phase | start | end | required skills), 3) Allocation Matrix (who is assigned where and at what %), 4) Utilization Dashboard (actual vs. capacity), 5) Conflict Detection (over-allocated resources highlighted).
description: Visualizes resource allocation across projects to prevent overcommitment and identify capacity gaps.
tips: Update weekly for accuracy. Flag anyone consistently above 90% utilization. Include planned PTO and training time in capacity calculations.

title: Industry Trend Analysis
prompt_text: Research current trends in [INDUSTRY] for [YEAR]. Structure findings as: 1) Top 5 Trends with evidence, 2) Market Size and Growth Projections, 3) Key Players and their strategies, 4) Emerging Technologies or Disruptions, 5) Regulatory Changes on the horizon, 6) Implications for [OUR_COMPANY].
description: Provides a structured industry overview for strategic planning and competitive awareness.
tips: Specify geographic scope and time horizon. Copilot searches across multiple sources. Cross-reference findings — no single source tells the full story.

title: Best Practice Research Brief
prompt_text: Research best practices for [TOPIC] across [INDUSTRY_OR_FUNCTION]. Deliver: 1) Summary of leading approaches (table: Organization | Approach | Key Features | Results), 2) Common Success Factors, 3) Common Pitfalls, 4) Applicability Assessment for our context, 5) Recommended adoption roadmap.
description: Synthesizes external best practices into actionable recommendations tailored to your context.
tips: Specify what success looks like for your organization. Copilot will filter practices by relevance. Include both aspirational leaders and comparable peers.

title: Regulatory Landscape Scan
prompt_text: Scan the regulatory landscape for [INDUSTRY_OR_DOMAIN] in [GEOGRAPHY]: 1) Current Regulations (table: Regulation | Scope | Key Requirements | Deadline), 2) Proposed/Pending Changes, 3) Enforcement Trends, 4) Peer Company Compliance Approaches, 5) Risk Assessment for our operations, 6) Recommended preparation steps.
description: Maps the regulatory environment to support compliance planning and risk assessment.
tips: Specify jurisdictions that matter. Copilot will search government sources and industry publications. Flag any deadlines within 12 months as urgent.

title: Technology Evaluation Research
prompt_text: Research [TECHNOLOGY_CATEGORY] solutions: 1) Market Overview and maturity, 2) Top Solutions Comparison (table: Solution | Strengths | Weaknesses | Pricing | Best For), 3) Analyst Ratings (Gartner/Forrester if available), 4) Customer Sentiment Summary, 5) Integration Considerations for our stack, 6) Recommendation.
description: Provides a comprehensive technology landscape assessment for procurement and architecture decisions.
tips: Specify must-have requirements and current technology stack. Copilot will filter and compare. Include total cost of ownership not just license fees.

title: Customer Sentiment Analysis
prompt_text: Research customer sentiment for [BRAND_OR_PRODUCT]: 1) Overall Sentiment Score and trend, 2) Positive Themes (what customers love), 3) Negative Themes (common complaints), 4) Competitive Sentiment Comparison, 5) Channel-by-Channel Breakdown (social/review sites/forums), 6) Recommended response strategy.
description: Aggregates customer voice data from multiple channels into actionable sentiment insights.
tips: Specify review platforms and social channels to monitor. Copilot will scan and categorize. Look for sentiment shifts over time not just point-in-time.

title: Labor Market Intelligence Report
prompt_text: Research the labor market for [ROLE_OR_FUNCTION] in [GEOGRAPHY]: 1) Supply/Demand Analysis, 2) Compensation Benchmarks (P25/P50/P75), 3) Top Employers Competing for this talent, 4) Emerging Skills and qualifications, 5) Remote Work Market Impact, 6) Sourcing Strategy Recommendations.
description: Provides data-driven talent market intelligence for hiring strategy and compensation planning.
tips: Specify experience level and key skills. Copilot will scan job boards and salary databases and LinkedIn trends. Update quarterly as market shifts rapidly.

title: Content Strategy Research
prompt_text: Research content performance in [INDUSTRY_OR_TOPIC]: 1) Top-performing content formats and channels, 2) Keyword/Topic Cluster Opportunities, 3) Competitor Content Analysis (table: Competitor | Content Type | Frequency | Engagement), 4) Audience Content Preferences, 5) Content Calendar Recommendations.
description: Informs content strategy with data on what works in your space and where gaps exist.
tips: Specify target audience and business goals. Copilot will analyze competitor content and audience signals. Focus on topics where you can add unique value.

title: Market Rent & Availability Research
prompt_text: Research commercial real estate market for [PROPERTY_TYPE] in [GEOGRAPHY]: 1) Market Overview (vacancy rate and absorption and new supply), 2) Rent Trends (asking rent and effective rent and concessions), 3) Comparable Properties (table: Address | Size | Rate | Amenities), 4) Submarket Rankings, 5) Forecast and timing recommendation.
description: Provides market intelligence for lease negotiations and site selection decisions.
tips: Specify size requirements and budget range. Copilot will scan listing databases and market reports. Compare to our current lease terms.

title: Supplier Market Intelligence
prompt_text: Research the supplier market for [CATEGORY]: 1) Market Structure (concentration and barriers to entry), 2) Key Suppliers (table: Supplier | Size | Specialization | Geography | Reputation), 3) Pricing Trends and cost drivers, 4) Innovation Leaders, 5) Risk Factors (geopolitical/supply chain), 6) Recommended shortlist.
description: Maps the supplier landscape for strategic sourcing and risk management.
tips: Specify volume requirements and quality standards. Copilot will scan industry databases and trade publications. Include regional alternatives for supply chain resilience.

title: Academic Research Summary
prompt_text: Summarize academic research on [TOPIC]: 1) Key Studies (table: Author/Year | Finding | Methodology | Sample Size), 2) Consensus Findings, 3) Methodological Limitations, 4) Practical Implications for [CONTEXT], 5) Gaps Requiring Further Research, 6) Recommended reading list.
description: Distills academic literature into practical business insights with proper source attribution.
tips: Specify the time period and key journals or databases. Copilot will summarize abstracts and findings. Note study limitations — not all research is equal.

title: Startup & Innovation Landscape Scan
prompt_text: Map the startup ecosystem for [DOMAIN]: 1) Emerging Players (table: Company | Founded | Funding | Focus | Stage), 2) Technology Approaches, 3) Funding Trends, 4) Partnership Opportunities, 5) Potential Threats to our business, 6) Recommended engagement strategy.
description: Identifies startup activity and innovation trends relevant to your strategic interests.
tips: Specify what makes a startup relevant (technology/market/capability). Copilot will scan Crunchbase and TechCrunch and industry sources. Update quarterly.

title: HR Onboarding Assistant Bot
prompt_text: Build a Copilot Studio bot that guides new hires through onboarding: 1) Welcome message with company overview, 2) Document checklist (tax forms/benefits enrollment/IT setup), 3) FAQ responses for common Day 1 questions, 4) Meeting scheduler for buddy/manager introductions, 5) Escalation to HR generalist for complex questions.
description: Automates the repetitive parts of onboarding while maintaining a personal touch for new employees.
tips: Use Adaptive Cards for document checklists. Connect to SharePoint for policy documents. Include a feedback loop to improve answers over time.

title: IT Helpdesk Triage Bot
prompt_text: Design an IT helpdesk triage bot: 1) Issue categorization (hardware/software/access/network), 2) Guided troubleshooting for common issues (password reset/VPN/printer), 3) Ticket creation with auto-populated fields, 4) Priority assignment based on impact and urgency, 5) Escalation routing to correct team, 6) Status update notifications.
description: Reduces ticket volume by resolving common issues automatically and routing complex ones correctly.
tips: Map your top 20 ticket types. Build troubleshooting trees for each. Connect to ServiceNow or Jira for ticket creation. Track deflection rate.

title: Customer Inquiry Router
prompt_text: Build a customer inquiry routing bot: 1) Intent detection (billing/support/sales/returns), 2) Information gathering (account number/order ID/issue description), 3) Knowledge base search for self-service answers, 4) Handoff to live agent with context transfer, 5) Post-interaction survey.
description: Routes customer inquiries to the right team with pre-gathered context to reduce handle time.
tips: Use Topics for intent detection. Connect to Dynamics or Salesforce for customer lookup. Always provide a human handoff option — do not trap customers in bot loops.

title: Approval Workflow Automation
prompt_text: Create an approval automation bot: 1) Request intake form (type/amount/justification), 2) Dynamic routing based on amount thresholds, 3) Approval notification to approver in Teams, 4) Reminder escalation if pending > [DAYS], 5) Status tracking and audit trail, 6) Notification to requester on decision.
description: Automates multi-level approval processes with tracking and escalation built in.
tips: Map your approval matrix (who approves what amounts). Use Power Automate flows for routing logic. Include an audit trail for compliance.

title: Knowledge Base Manager Bot
prompt_text: Design a knowledge base assistant: 1) Natural language search across SharePoint/Confluence, 2) Context-aware answers with source citations, 3) Was this helpful? feedback loop, 4) Gap detection (log questions without answers), 5) Content suggestion for knowledge gaps, 6) Analytics dashboard on usage patterns.
description: Provides instant access to organizational knowledge while identifying gaps that need documentation.
tips: Start with a focused knowledge domain. Measure answer quality through user feedback. Use gap detection to prioritize new content creation.

title: Automated Report Generator Bot
prompt_text: Build a reporting bot that: 1) Accepts report requests via natural language (what report/what period/what filters), 2) Queries data sources (SQL/SharePoint/API), 3) Generates formatted output (table/chart/summary), 4) Delivers via Teams or email, 5) Schedules recurring reports, 6) Handles ad-hoc drill-down questions.
description: Democratizes data access by letting anyone request reports in plain language without knowing SQL or BI tools.
tips: Start with 3-5 most requested reports. Build templates for each. Add new reports based on usage patterns. Always include data freshness timestamp.

title: Procurement Request Bot
prompt_text: Create a procurement request bot: 1) Request intake (what/why/when/budget), 2) Catalog search for existing contracts, 3) Policy check (is this within guidelines), 4) Auto-routing based on category and amount, 5) Supplier recommendation from preferred list, 6) Status tracking with Teams notifications.
description: Streamlines procurement requests by checking policies and preferred suppliers before routing for approval.
tips: Connect to your contract management system. Map category managers for routing. Include a policy FAQ to reduce invalid requests.

title: Lead Qualification Bot
prompt_text: Build a lead qualification chatbot: 1) Conversational lead capture (name/company/need), 2) BANT qualification (Budget/Authority/Need/Timeline), 3) Lead scoring based on responses, 4) Dynamic content delivery based on interest, 5) CRM integration for lead creation, 6) Handoff to sales rep with qualification notes.
description: Qualifies inbound leads 24/7 and passes only qualified leads to sales with full context.
tips: Design the conversation flow to feel natural not interrogative. Score leads on explicit signals not assumptions. Always offer a human contact option.

title: Facility Request & Booking Bot
prompt_text: Create a facility management bot: 1) Room/space booking with availability check, 2) Maintenance request submission (category/urgency/location), 3) Visitor registration and badge prep, 4) Parking allocation, 5) Catering request for events, 6) Status tracking for all requests.
description: Consolidates facility-related requests into a single conversational interface for employees.
tips: Connect to your room booking system and facilities management tool. Include building maps or floor plans. Prioritize safety requests.

title: Exception Handling Bot
prompt_text: Build an exception handling bot: 1) Exception request intake (policy/reason/duration), 2) Policy lookup and display of current rule, 3) Risk assessment questions, 4) Auto-approve for low-risk pre-defined exceptions, 5) Route high-risk exceptions to appropriate approver, 6) Expiry tracking and renewal reminders.
description: Standardizes exception handling while maintaining governance controls and audit trail.
tips: Define which exceptions can be auto-approved. Set maximum duration for all exceptions. Build in automatic expiry — no permanent exceptions without review.

title: KPI Dashboard Builder
prompt_text: Build a KPI dashboard analyzing [DATASET]: 1) Define top 10 KPIs with calculation logic, 2) Current Period Performance vs. Target, 3) Trend Analysis (MoM and QoQ and YoY), 4) Drill-down by [DIMENSIONS] (region/product/team), 5) Alert Thresholds (red/yellow/green), 6) Executive summary of findings.
description: Creates an interactive KPI framework from raw data with automated status indicators.
tips: Define what green/yellow/red means for each KPI before building. Include leading indicators alongside lagging ones. Update targets quarterly.

title: Customer Churn Analysis
prompt_text: Analyze customer churn in [DATASET]: 1) Overall churn rate and trend, 2) Churn by segment (table: Segment | Churn Rate | Revenue Impact | Top Reason), 3) Early warning signals (behavioral patterns before churn), 4) Survival Analysis (time-to-churn distribution), 5) Retention Intervention Recommendations ranked by impact.
description: Identifies churn patterns and early warning indicators to enable proactive retention strategies.
tips: Include customer activity data (logins/usage/support tickets) alongside account data. Look for behavioral changes 30-60-90 days before churn.

title: Revenue Attribution Analysis
prompt_text: Analyze revenue drivers in [DATASET]: 1) Revenue Decomposition (by product/channel/region/customer segment), 2) Growth Contribution Analysis (what drove YoY change), 3) Price vs. Volume vs. Mix Effects, 4) Cohort Revenue Analysis, 5) Revenue Concentration Risk (top 10 customers as % of total), 6) Forecast implications.
description: Decomposes revenue into component drivers to identify what is actually driving growth or decline.
tips: Include transaction-level data if possible. Copilot will aggregate and decompose. Watch for concentration risk — if top 10 customers are more than 30% of revenue flag it.

title: Operational Efficiency Metrics
prompt_text: Analyze operational efficiency in [DATASET]: 1) Throughput Metrics (volume/time/cost per unit), 2) Bottleneck Identification, 3) Capacity Utilization, 4) Quality Metrics (error rate/rework/defects), 5) Benchmark Comparison (internal teams or industry), 6) Improvement Opportunities ranked by ROI.
description: Identifies operational inefficiencies and quantifies improvement opportunities from process data.
tips: Include timestamps for process steps to identify bottlenecks. Compare across teams or time periods. Focus on the constraint — improving non-bottlenecks does not help.

title: Employee Analytics & Workforce Insights
prompt_text: Analyze workforce data in [DATASET]: 1) Headcount Overview (by department/level/tenure), 2) Turnover Analysis (voluntary vs. involuntary and by segment), 3) Diversity Metrics, 4) Compensation Distribution, 5) Engagement Score Correlation Analysis, 6) Flight Risk Indicators.
description: Provides people analytics insights to inform HR strategy and workforce planning decisions.
tips: Anonymize data before analysis. Look for correlations between engagement scores and turnover. Flag teams with turnover 2x company average.

title: Recruiting Funnel Analytics
prompt_text: Analyze recruiting funnel data: 1) Funnel Metrics (table: Stage | Volume | Conversion Rate | Time in Stage), 2) Source Effectiveness (applications and hires and cost per hire by source), 3) Time-to-Fill Analysis by role type, 4) Offer Acceptance Rate and decline reasons, 5) Quality of Hire indicators, 6) Recommendations.
description: Optimizes recruiting by identifying funnel bottlenecks and high-performing sourcing channels.
tips: Export ATS data for the period. Include cost data for ROI calculations. Compare conversion rates against industry benchmarks.

title: Campaign Performance Deep Dive
prompt_text: Analyze campaign data for [CAMPAIGN]: 1) Performance Summary (impressions/clicks/conversions/revenue), 2) Channel Breakdown with efficiency metrics, 3) Audience Segment Performance, 4) Creative/Message Testing Results, 5) Funnel Drop-off Analysis, 6) Optimization Recommendations with projected impact.
description: Provides granular campaign analysis to optimize spend allocation and creative strategy.
tips: Include all touchpoint data for accurate attribution. Compare against benchmarks and prior campaigns. Test one variable at a time for clean learnings.

title: Portfolio Performance Analysis
prompt_text: Analyze CRE portfolio data: 1) Occupancy and Utilization by location, 2) Cost per Seat/Sq Ft Comparison, 3) Lease Expiration Profile, 4) Energy Efficiency Rankings, 5) Employee Satisfaction by Location, 6) Consolidation Opportunities with savings estimate.
description: Provides a data-driven portfolio assessment to optimize space and reduce costs across locations.
tips: Include badge data for utilization and lease terms for cost analysis. Compare against industry benchmarks. Model work-from-home impact on space needs.

title: Procurement Savings Tracker
prompt_text: Analyze procurement savings data: 1) Savings by Category (table: Category | Target | Achieved | Variance), 2) Savings Type Breakdown (negotiated/demand reduction/specification change), 3) Savings Realization Rate, 4) Pipeline of In-Progress Initiatives, 5) Trend vs. Prior Periods, 6) Recommendations for remaining target.
description: Tracks and validates procurement savings against targets with visibility into realization rates.
tips: Define savings calculation methodology upfront. Track both hard savings (invoice reduction) and soft savings (cost avoidance). Include validation status.

title: Survey Results Analyzer
prompt_text: Analyze survey results in [DATASET]: 1) Response Rate and demographics, 2) Score Summary (table: Question | Mean | Median | Distribution), 3) Top Scores and Bottom Scores, 4) Open-Text Theme Analysis, 5) Segment Comparison (by department/tenure/role), 6) Year-over-Year Trends, 7) Priority Actions based on impact analysis.
description: Transforms raw survey data into actionable insights with statistical rigor and thematic analysis.
tips: Include demographic fields for segmentation. Copilot will calculate statistical significance for group differences. Focus actions on items with high importance and low scores.

title: Data Privacy Guardian
prompt_text: Custom Instruction: When I share documents or data, always: 1) Flag any PII or sensitive data before processing, 2) Suggest redaction where appropriate, 3) Never include customer names or account numbers in summaries, 4) Remind me of data classification if content appears confidential, 5) Default to internal-only classification unless told otherwise.
description: Adds a data privacy layer to all Copilot interactions to prevent accidental data exposure.
tips: Set this as a persistent custom instruction. Review monthly to align with updated privacy policies. Combine with DLP policies for comprehensive protection.

title: Executive Communication Style
prompt_text: Custom Instruction: Format all my outputs for executive audiences: 1) Lead with the conclusion or recommendation, 2) Maximum 1 page unless I specify longer, 3) Use tables over paragraphs for data, 4) Include a clear Ask or Decision Needed section, 5) Bold key numbers and dates, 6) End with Next Steps and owners.
description: Forces all Copilot output into concise executive-ready format automatically.
tips: Combine with department-specific instructions for maximum impact. Override with specific formatting when needed for different audiences.

title: Project Management Enforcer
prompt_text: Custom Instruction: For all project-related requests: 1) Always include Owner/DRI for each action item, 2) Include specific dates not vague timelines, 3) Flag dependencies and risks, 4) Use RAG status (Red/Amber/Green) for status updates, 5) Ask me for missing information rather than assuming, 6) Track against previously stated milestones.
description: Adds project management rigor to all Copilot interactions automatically.
tips: Set at the beginning of a project. Update milestones as they change. Works best when combined with a project charter or scope document.

title: Brand Voice Consistency
prompt_text: Custom Instruction: All content I create should follow our brand voice: 1) Tone: [SPECIFY - e.g. professional yet approachable], 2) Avoid: [SPECIFY - e.g. jargon/passive voice/exclamation marks], 3) Always: [SPECIFY - e.g. use active voice/include call to action], 4) Formatting: [SPECIFY - e.g. short paragraphs/bullet points for features], 5) Terminology: Use [PREFERRED_TERMS] instead of [AVOIDED_TERMS].
description: Ensures all Copilot-generated content adheres to brand guidelines without manual review.
tips: Fill in your specific brand voice attributes. Share with the marketing team for consistency. Review quarterly as brand evolves.

title: Financial Analysis Standards
prompt_text: Custom Instruction: For all financial analysis: 1) Show numbers in thousands with one decimal (e.g. $1.2K), 2) Always include YoY and QoQ comparisons, 3) Use RAG indicators for variance (Green: within 5%, Yellow: 5-10%, Red: >10%), 4) Include source data reference, 5) Flag assumptions explicitly, 6) Default currency: [SPECIFY].
description: Standardizes financial output format for consistency across all Copilot-generated analysis.
tips: Align thresholds with your company's materiality standards. Share with finance team for adoption. Update currency and formatting for regional teams.

title: Meeting Productivity Protocol
prompt_text: Custom Instruction: For all meeting-related tasks: 1) Agenda items must have time allocations, 2) Notes must capture decisions separately from discussion, 3) Action items must have owner and deadline, 4) Flag agenda items that could be an email instead, 5) Follow-up summaries sent within 2 hours, 6) Track carryover items from previous meetings.
description: Brings structure and accountability to meeting workflows through consistent Copilot behavior.
tips: Apply to Teams meeting recaps. Works best when meeting organizers adopt the same standard. Review action items at the start of the next meeting.

title: Quality Assurance Checker
prompt_text: Custom Instruction: Before finalizing any output, automatically check: 1) Spelling and grammar, 2) Consistency of formatting (headers/bullets/numbering), 3) Accuracy of any calculations or numbers, 4) Completeness (are all requested sections present), 5) Tone appropriateness for the stated audience, 6) Flag any claims that need verification.
description: Adds an automatic quality check layer to all Copilot outputs before delivery.
tips: Enable as a persistent instruction. Copilot will self-review before presenting output. Reduces review cycles and catches common errors.

title: Regulatory Compliance Filter
prompt_text: Custom Instruction: Apply these compliance checks to all content: 1) Flag language that could be construed as a guarantee or promise, 2) Ensure disclaimers are present for [SPECIFY - e.g. financial/medical/legal content], 3) Check for inclusive language, 4) Flag any references to competitors by name, 5) Ensure all claims have supporting evidence, 6) Default to conservative/cautious framing for external communications.
description: Automatically screens Copilot output for common compliance and regulatory risks.
tips: Customize for your industry regulations. Have compliance team review and approve the instruction set. Update when regulations change.
